title: Chronic diarrhea with weight loss

chart.oneLiner: 35F presents with months of loose, bulky, foul-smelling stools, bloating, fatigue, and unintentional weight loss.

opening: I've had loose stools and bloating for months, I'm tired all the time, and I've lost weight.
patientFile:
PERSONA: 35-year-old woman, thin, fatigued. Good historian.

HPI: Several months of loose, bulky, greasy/foul-smelling stools (steatorrhea), bloating, and excessive gas; symptoms worse with bread/pasta (only if asked about diet/triggers). Fatigue and ~12 lb unintentional weight loss. An itchy, blistering rash on the elbows/knees (dermatitis herpetiformis) — only if asked or examined. NO nocturnal bloody diarrhea, NO recent travel, NO antibiotics.
PMH: prior iron-deficiency anemia not explained; possible autoimmune thyroid disease. FAMILY: a sister with celiac disease (if asked). MEDS: none. SOCIAL: minimal alcohol.

PHYSICAL EXAM (report ONLY what is requested; give normals when asked):
- General: thin, pale.
- Vitals: BP 112/72, HR 84, RR 14, 36.8°C, SpO2 99% RA.
- Abdomen: soft, mildly distended, nontender; active bowel sounds.
- Skin: grouped vesicles/excoriations on extensor surfaces if examined. Angular cheilitis if examined.

HIDDEN RULES:
- NEVER state or hint at a diagnosis. Answer only what is asked.
- Do NOT volunteer the gluten/diet trigger, the rash, or the family history unless asked about diet/triggers, skin symptoms, or family history.
- If asked something not in this file, give a reasonable normal/negative answer.

labs.CBC: Hgb 10.8 with low MCV — iron-deficiency-pattern anemia.
labs.Serology: Tissue transglutaminase IgA (tTG-IgA) strongly POSITIVE; total IgA normal (so the IgA-based test is valid).
labs.Nutrition: Low ferritin, low vitamin D, mildly low folate — malabsorption pattern.
labs.Biopsy: Duodenal biopsy (on a gluten-containing diet): villous atrophy, crypt hyperplasia, intraepithelial lymphocytosis — consistent with celiac disease.

ekg.label: 12-lead EKG
ekg.findings: Abnormally short QTc (~330 ms) with tall peaked T waves.
ekg.verify: Source a study matching: Abnormally short QTc (~330 ms) with tall peaked T waves. Self-host with appropriate credit; do not reuse the prior normal tracing.
ekg.attribution: LITFL ECG Library (litfl.com) — © Life in the Fast Lane, CC BY-NC-SA 4.0. Educational/not-for-profit use, credit litfl.com; skip any image labelled third-party.

cxr.label: Chest X-ray
cxr.findings: Diffuse bilateral reticulonodular interstitial opacities (chronic interstitial lung disease).
cxr.verify: Source a study matching: Diffuse bilateral reticulonodular interstitial opacities (chronic interstitial lung disease). Self-host with appropriate credit; do not reuse the prior normal tracing.
cxr.imageDescription: chest radiograph showing chronic interstitial lung disease

steps.label: Differential
steps.prompt: Based on your history and physical, list your differential for this chronic diarrhea (note your leading diagnosis).
steps.rubric: Celiac disease (leading,7). Irritable bowel syndrome (core,3). Inflammatory bowel disease (core,3). Lactose/carbohydrate intolerance (core,2). Pancreatic exocrine insufficiency (core,3). Microscopic colitis (bonus,2). Connects chronic steatorrhea + weight loss + unexplained iron deficiency to a malabsorptive process / celiac (core,5).
steps.idealAnswer: Leading diagnosis: Celiac disease. Must-not-miss diagnoses to exclude: none specific. Also reasonable: Irritable bowel syndrome; Inflammatory bowel disease; Lactose/carbohydrate intolerance; Pancreatic exocrine insufficiency.

coreActions.item: Connects chronic steatorrhea + weight loss + unexplained iron deficiency to a malabsorptive process / celiac

steps.label: Workup
steps.prompt: What initial workup do you want to order?
steps.rubric: Celiac serology — tTG-IgA WITH total IgA, while the patient is still eating gluten (CANT-MISS,5). CBC and iron studies (3). Nutritional labs — ferritin, vitamin D, folate, B12 (2). Upper endoscopy with duodenal biopsy to confirm (3). Stool studies to exclude infection (2). PENALTY: starting a gluten-free diet BEFORE testing (causes false-negative serology/biopsy) (-3).
steps.idealAnswer: Order: EKG; CXR; CBC; Serology; Nutrition; Biopsy; Stool studies.

criticalActions.item: Celiac serology — tTG-IgA WITH total IgA, while the patient is still eating gluten

coreActions.item: Nutritional labs — ferritin, vitamin D, folate, B12
Upper endoscopy with duodenal biopsy to confirm

penalties.item: starting a gluten-free diet BEFORE testing (causes false-negative serology/biopsy)

requiredTests: Celiac serology — tTG-IgA WITH total IgA, while the patient is still eating gluten
contraindicatedTests: starting a gluten-free diet BEFORE testing (causes false-negative serology/biopsy)
orderSensitiveActions: starting a gluten-free diet BEFORE testing (causes false-negative serology/biopsy)

steps.label: Read EKG
steps.prompt: Here is the patient's 12-lead EKG. Describe your findings.
steps.rubric: Findings = abnormally short QT interval (QTc ~330 ms) with tall peaked T waves. CREDIT: measures and identifies a short QTc (7); recognizes short QT as an arrhythmia substrate (5); systematic read (3). PENALTY: not measuring the QT and calling the tracing normal (-2).
keyFindings: recognizes short QT as an arrhythmia substrate
commonMistakes: not measuring QT and calling the tracing normal
steps.idealAnswer: Short QT interval (QTc ~330 ms) with tall T waves.

coreActions: recognizes short QT as an arrhythmia substrate

penalties.label: calls the study normal / misses the abnormality

steps.label: Read CXR
steps.prompt: Here is the patient's chest X-ray. Describe your findings.
steps.rubric: Findings = diffuse bilateral reticulonodular interstitial opacities (chronic interstitial lung disease). CREDIT: identifies the diffuse reticulonodular pattern (6); recognizes a chronic interstitial process rather than acute infection (4). PENALTY: calling the film normal or labelling the diffuse chronic changes an acute infection (-2).
commonMistakes: calling diffuse chronic changes an acute infection
steps.idealAnswer: Chronic interstitial lung disease: diffuse bilateral reticulonodular opacities.

penalties.label: calls the study normal / misses the abnormality

steps.label: Revised Dx
steps.prompt: Positive tTG-IgA with normal total IgA and duodenal villous atrophy on biopsy. What is your diagnosis?
steps.rubric: Celiac disease (6). Cites positive serology with a valid IgA level plus confirmatory villous atrophy on biopsy; notes the dermatitis herpetiformis association (4).
steps.idealAnswer: Celiac disease. Confirmed by positive tTG-IgA with a valid (normal) total IgA plus duodenal villous atrophy on biopsy; the extensor-surface rash supports the dermatitis herpetiformis association.

steps.label: Management
steps.rubric: Per MGH diarrhea / malabsorption (celiac): lifelong strict gluten-free diet with dietitian referral (CANT-MISS,8); confirm diagnosis – tTG-IgA + biopsy before diet (5); replete deficiencies – iron/B12/folate/vit D/calcium (4); screen for associated autoimmune disease and bone density (3); monitor response (serology/symptoms) (2). PENALTY: starting a gluten-free diet before confirmatory testing (-2); immunosuppression for uncomplicated celiac (-2).
steps.idealAnswer: Lifelong strict gluten-free diet is the treatment – refer to a dietitian. Confirm with serology (tTG-IgA with total IgA) and duodenal biopsy before committing to the diet. Replete deficiencies (iron, B12, folate, vitamin D, calcium). Screen for associated conditions (thyroid, type 1 DM) and osteoporosis. Monitor serologies/symptoms for response.

coreActions.item: Screen for associated disease and osteoporosis

optionalActions: Screen for associated disease and osteoporosis

caseSummary.diagnosticFramework: 35F presents with months of loose, bulky, foul-smelling stools, bloating, fatigue, and unintentional weight loss. The diagnosis is Celiac disease, reached by eliciting the focused history, performing the targeted exam, and interpreting the directed workup while excluding the must-not-miss alternatives.
keyHistoryClues: Itchy, blistering rash on elbows/knees (dermatitis herpetiformis)
keyPhysicalExamFindings: Abdomen: soft, mildly distended, nontender; active bowel sounds.
Abdomen: soft, mildly distended, nontender; active bowel sounds.
reasoningPathway: Frame the presentation from the chart and vitals.
Elicit the focused history, including sensitive items only on direct questioning.
Perform the targeted physical exam maneuvers to reveal key findings.
Build a differential led by Celiac disease while explicitly excluding must-not-miss causes.
Order a focused, order-sensitive workup and interpret EKG/CXR and labs.
Commit to Celiac disease and manage with stabilization, definitive treatment, consults, and disposition.
criticalWorkupItems: Celiac serology — tTG-IgA WITH total IgA, while the patient is still eating gluten
imageInterpretationPearls: An incidental abnormality is an interpretation task in its own right — name it, then judge its relevance.
Read every study systematically before committing.
State pertinent negatives, not just positives.

items.item: Shows empathy for the patient's concern
Explains next steps in patient-friendly language
